LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BLUE & GOLD FLEET, L.P.'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS CGC-17-559403 AND CGC-16-552372. THE CAUSE IS SUBMITTED BY BOTH SIDES WITHOUT ARGUMENT. THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO A DIFFERENT TRIAL DATE. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION. GRANTED AND THE TRIAL DATE IS CONTINUED TO JULY 16, 2018. THE TWO CASES ARE CONSOLIDATED FOR ALL PURPOSES AND THE LEAD CASE IS 552372. ALL FURTHER FILINGS ARE IN THE LEAD CASE ONLY. THE CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE SET IN 559403 ON JANUARY 3, 2018 AND THE HEARING ON THE MOTION TO AMEND SET IN 552372 ON NOVEMBER 28, 2017 ARE VACATED. BLUE & GOLD HAS SHOWN GOOD CAUSE FOR THE CONSOLIDATION AND GRANTING THE MOTION ADVANCES JUDICIAL ECONOMY. THE ONLY OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION IS BASED ON THE IMMINENCE OF THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE. A FOUR MONTH CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL SHOULD PROVIDE AMPLE TIME FOR THE PARTIES TO ASSIMILATE THE CLAIMS AGAINST THE MARITIME ACADEMY INTO THE FIRST FILED CASE. ALL DISCOVERY AND OTHER DATES ARE TRIGGERED OFF THE NEW TRIAL DATE. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-559403 WITH CASE CGC-16-552372
Off Calendar Nov-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Lia T. Marks of the law offices of Lyle C. Cavin, Jr. (510-444-2501) appears as counsel for plaintiff in 552372, Christopher Taylor

Attorney, Christopher W Goodroe of the law offices of C W Goodroe (510-969-2369) appears as counsel for plaintiff in 559403, Golden Gate Bridge Highway & Transportaion District

Attorney, Francesca M. Lanpher of the law offices of Keesal Young & Logan (415-398-6000) appears as counsel for defendant in 552372 and 559403, Board of Trustees of CSU

Attorney, Matthew W.J. Johnston of the law offices of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (415-438-6676) appears as counsel for defendants in 552372, Golden Gate Bridge Highway & Transportaion District

Attorney, Chelsea D. Yuan of the law offices of Gibson Robb & Lindh LLP (415-348-6000 appears as counsel for defendant in 552372, Strategic Operations

The cause is submitted by both sides without argument as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The parties stipulate to a different trial date. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANT BLUE & GOLD FLEET, L.P.'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS CGC-17-559403 and CGC-16-552372 is GRANTED and the trial date is continued to July 16, 2018. The two cases are consolidated for all purposes and the lead case is 552372. All further filings are in the lead case only. The case management conference set in 559403 on January 3, 2018 and the hearing on the motion to amend set in 552372 on November 28, 2017 are vacated. Blue & Gold has shown good cause for the consolidation and granting the motion advances judicial economy. The only opposition to the motion is based on the imminence of the current trial date. A four month continuance of the trial should provide ample time for the parties to assimilate the claims against the Maritime Academy into the first filed case. All discovery and other dates are triggered off the new trial date. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)
